meaning: n.(跛子用的)拐杖,支撑,帮助vt.支撑
examples: He gave up the crutch of alcohol.
Ooh, hand me that crutch, would you?
The hat and crutch are also dancing.
Is Mousemail.com nothing more than a high-tech crutch, a state-of-the-art substitute for old-fashioned good parenting?
As a clear concession to her frailty, she's had the younger Sheryl Crow along as a crutch.
